commit.message:
[#176] Replace lightbox with Spotlight.js for docs image lightbox (#330)

Implements #176.

The original lightbox2 library renders SVG images oversized. This PR
replaces it with
[Spotlight.js](https://github.com/nextapps-de/spotlight), a lightweight,
dependency-free modern lightbox library.

## Summary

- Replace `sphinxcontrib-lightbox2` with a new `sphinxcontrib-spotlight`
Sphinx extension
- The extension bundles Spotlight.js v0.7.8 (single 22KB bundle, vs 70KB
for glightbox)
- All documentation images are automatically wrapped in `<a
class="spotlight">` anchors via a Sphinx post-transform
- Images can be excluded per-image with the `no-spotlight` CSS class

## Changes

**New package** `py_packages/sphinxcontrib_spotlight/`:
- Bundles Spotlight.js v0.7.8 as a single self-contained JS bundle
(includes CSS)
- Custom `spotlight_reference` node defers URI resolution to write time,
ensuring hrefs point to `_images/` output paths rather than source paths
- `SpotlightTransform` post-transform wraps all `image` nodes
automatically
- Alt text becomes `data-description` for the lightbox caption
- Spotlight auto-initializes from markup — no manual JS init script
needed

**Updated configuration:**
- `docs/source/conf.py`: switched extension from
`sphinxcontrib.lightbox2` → `sphinxcontrib.spotlight`, removed
`lightbox2_*` config options
- `pyproject.toml`: updated dependency and uv editable source

## Testing

- [x] Sphinx build completes without errors
- [x] Extension loads correctly in the docs venv
(`sphinxcontrib.spotlight` importable)
- [x] Old `sphinxcontrib-glightbox` and `sphinxcontrib-lightbox2`
packages removed from venv

---------

Signed-off-by: Anton Matosov <anton.matosov@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: anton-matosov <3339485+anton-matosov@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Slice shape inference crashes with SIGABRT on empty dimensions (#7738)

Fix #7735 

This pull request addresses issues with the ONNX Slice operator when
handling empty dimensions, ensuring correct and safe shape inference in
these edge cases. The main focus is to prevent undefined behavior and
ensure that slicing on empty dimensions consistently produces outputs
with zero length, regardless of the step direction. Additional
regression tests are included to verify these fixes, and several test
data files are updated to reflect the new behavior.

**Slice operator improvements for empty dimensions:**

* Updated the `processSliceInputs` function in `defs.cc` to
short-circuit and produce a zero-length output when the input dimension
size is zero, avoiding invalid clamp bounds and potential undefined
behavior.
* Modified shape inference logic in `old.cc` to detect empty dimensions
and set the output dimension value to zero, both for standard and
expanded shape inference code paths.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-5e200991755406044e2dbb2449683bb5c6d77583d1459f0065a756813dd1b59bR2635-R2645)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-5e200991755406044e2dbb2449683bb5c6d77583d1459f0065a756813dd1b59bR6329-R6335)

**Testing and regression coverage:**

* Added two new tests in `shape_inference_test.py` to verify that
slicing on empty dimensions with both positive and negative steps
produces the correct output shape, preventing regression of issue #7735.

**Test data updates:**

* Updated several `output_0.pb` binary test data files under
`onnx/backend/test/data/node/` (including attention, gelu,
hammingwindow, and lppool tests) to align with the corrected Slice
operator behavior on empty dimensions.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-875055c843db41ea7d579c8bd29201e6f60bb4175ac96f840bdd27d488866198L1-R7)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-d6fe5631baa8b6cb320c03143f797abe3dce4ddf5363fa9cfe71cc12bc31ac53L2-R3)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-3275d5e3517a0e95967de3c46a75b02104980f2566f1a0cbefa386ea8e02f312L1-R3)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-537fe44987ef880d522c9c88283070745874abc990951f49eff8dccc35630af1L2-R2)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-3d57e2f0cf63b32349f6619a2f61e90d4a4aa4fdac9a794af6bfb00c97fe9f8eL1-R2)

These changes collectively improve the robustness of ONNX shape
inference and operator correctness when handling tensors with empty
dimensions.

---------

Signed-off-by: Ti-Tai Wang <titaiwang@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(presets): Pluggable preset system with catalog, resolver, and skills propagation (#1787)

* Initial plan

* feat(templates): add pluggable template system with packs, catalog, resolver, and CLI commands

Co-authored-by: mnriem <15701806+mnriem@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(templates): add comprehensive unit tests for template pack system

Co-authored-by: mnriem <15701806+mnriem@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(presets): pluggable preset system with template/command overrides, catalog, and resolver

- Rename 'template packs' to 'presets' to avoid naming collision with core templates
- PresetManifest, PresetRegistry, PresetManager, PresetCatalog, PresetResolver in presets.py
- Extract CommandRegistrar to agents.py as shared infrastructure
- CLI: specify preset list/add/remove/search/resolve/info
- CLI: specify preset catalog list/add/remove
- --preset option on specify init
- Priority-based preset stacking (--priority, lower = higher precedence)
- Command overrides registered into all detected agent directories (17+ agents)
- Extension command safety: skip registration if target extension not installed
- Multi-catalog support: env var, project config, user config, built-in defaults
- resolve_template() / Resolve-Template in bash/PowerShell scripts
- Self-test preset: overrides all 6 core templates + 1 command
- Scaffold with 4 examples: core/extension template and command overrides
- Preset catalog (catalog.json, catalog.community.json)
- Documentation: README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, PUBLISHING.md
- 110 preset tests, 253 total tests passing

* feat(presets): propagate command overrides to skills via init-options

- Add save_init_options() / load_init_options() helpers that persist
  CLI flags from 'specify init' to .specify/init-options.json
- PresetManager._register_skills() overwrites SKILL.md files when
  --ai-skills was used during init and corresponding skill dirs exist
- PresetManager._unregister_skills() restores core template content
  on preset removal
- registered_skills stored in preset registry metadata
- 8 new tests covering skill override, skip conditions, and restore

* fix: address PR check failures (ruff F541, CodeQL URL substring)

- Remove extraneous f-prefix from two f-strings without placeholders
- Replace substring URL check in test with startswith/endswith assertions
  to satisfy CodeQL incomplete URL substring sanitization rule

* fix: address Copilot PR review comments

- Move save_init_options() before preset install so skills propagation
  works during 'specify init --preset --ai-skills'
- Clean up downloaded ZIP after successful preset install during init
- Validate --from URL scheme (require HTTPS, HTTP only for localhost)
- Expose unregister_commands() on extensions.py CommandRegistrar wrapper
  instead of reaching into private _registrar field
- Use _get_merged_packs() for search() and get_pack_info() so all
  active catalogs are searched, not just the highest-priority one
- Fix fetch_catalog() cache to verify cached URL matches current URL
- Fix PresetResolver: script resolution uses .sh extension, consistent
  file extensions throughout resolve(), and resolve_with_source()
  delegates to resolve() to honor template_type parameter
- Fix bash common.sh: fall through to directory scan when python3
  returns empty preset list
- Fix PowerShell Resolve-Template: filter out dot-folders and sort
  extensions deterministically

* fix: narrow empty except blocks and add explanatory comments

* fix: address Copilot PR review comments (round 2)

- Fix init --preset error masking: distinguish "not found" from real errors
- Fix bash resolve_template: skip hidden dirs in extensions (match Python/PS)
- Fix temp dir leaks in tests: use temp_dir fixture instead of mkdtemp
- Fix self-test catalog entry: add note that it's local-only (no download_url)
- Fix Windows path issue in resolve_with_source: use Path.relative_to()
- Fix skill restore path: use project's .specify/templates/commands/ not source tree
- Add encoding="utf-8" to all file read/write in agents.py
- Update test to set up core command templates for skill restoration

* fix: remove self-test from catalog.json (local-only preset)

* fix: address Copilot PR review comments (round 3)

- Fix PS Resolve-Template fallback to skip dot-prefixed dirs (.cache)
- Rename _catalog to _catalog_name for consistency with extension system
- Enforce install_allowed policy in CLI preset add and download_pack()
- Fix shell injection: pass registry path via env var instead of string interpolation

* fix: correct PresetError docstring from template to preset

* Removed CHANGELOG requirement

* Applying review recommendations

* Applying review recommendations

* Applying review recommendations

* Applying review recommendations

commit.message:
test: add coverage tests for parallel and workflow modules

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Forms: Create synced form when inserting form variations via block inserter (#47335)

* Forms: Create synced form when inserting form variations via block inserter

When users insert a form variation directly from the block inserter (e.g.,
"Contact Form"), WordPress creates the block with innerBlocks immediately,
bypassing the VariationPicker. This meant synced forms were not being created.

This adds a new hook `useCreateSyncedFormOnInsertion` that detects when a form
block is inserted with inner blocks but without a ref attribute, and
automatically creates a synced form when central form management is enabled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Forms: Use variationName attribute and wasBlockJustInserted for synced form creation

Simplify the useCreateSyncedFormOnInsertion hook by:
- Using wasBlockJustInserted selector to detect newly inserted blocks
- Using variationName attribute directly instead of deriving variation from inner blocks
- Removing complex heuristic-based variation detection logic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Forms: Address review feedback for synced form creation hook

- Add entity preloading before setting ref to prevent skeleton flash
- Replace unstable useEffect deps with empty array and ref guard
- Remove unnecessary registry.batch() wrapper around single dispatch
- Update getVariationTitle docblock to accurately describe return cases
- Replace flaky setTimeout in tests with deterministic microtask flushing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Forms: Simplify synced form creation hook for performance

- Short-circuit useSelect after creation attempt to stop unnecessary store queries
- Collapse guard clauses into single shouldCreate boolean
- Match variations by name instead of attributes.variationName
- Add variationName to variations missing it so name-based lookup works
- Simplify tests by removing redundant mock data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Forms: Fix TypeScript error for currentPostId type mismatch

getCurrentPostId() returns string | number, but createSyncedForm
expects number. Wrap with Number() to satisfy the type checker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use variationName in insertion logic

Only create a synced form on insertion if the block has a variationName: add a truthy attributes.variationName check to the shouldCreate condition and include it in the dependency array. Also fix the variation title lookup to compare against variations' attributes.variationName (not v.name) so the correct form title is selected.

* Prevent post-unmount updates in form insertion hook

Add cancellation handling and safer entity preload in useCreateSyncedFormOnInsertion: introduce a cancelled flag with a cleanup return to avoid setting attributes or showing notices after unmount, wrap getEntityRecord in try/catch as a best-effort preload, and bail early when cancelled. Update tests to cover preload failure (still sets ref), adjust variationName test data, ensure no synced form is created when variationName is missing, and add a test for unknown variationName fallback behavior.

* Forms: Add useEffect dependencies to fix synced form creation for variations

The empty dependency array meant the effect only ran on mount. If
shouldCreate was false initially (e.g. innerBlocks not yet populated),
the synced form would never be created. The hasAttemptedCreation ref
prevents double execution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Reset creation flag on cleanup and silence deps

Reset hasAttemptedCreation.current to false in the effect cleanup so the creation attempt flag is cleared when the component is unmounted. The effect's dependency array was replaced with an empty array and an eslint-disable comment was added to suppress react-hooks/exhaustive-deps; this makes the effect run only once on mount (intentional to avoid repeated creation attempts). Previously the effect listed shouldCreate, attributes, innerBlocks, currentPostId, setAttributes, createSuccessNotice, and createErrorNotice as dependencies.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add print button at bottom of each month page

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Redesign month calendars: better styling, landscape print, mobile-friendly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Step10 research encounters (#25)

* fix: crafted card increments existing library entry instead of duplicating

Crafting now adds +1 to the library count of the existing card rather than
creating a new Library entry. Added Library::increment_library_count() helper.
Updated both conclude_crafting_encounter and auto_conclude_craft to use it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: merge conclude/auto_conclude craft into shared finish_active_craft

Extract shared logic (check affordability → pay costs → add crafted card) into
a single finish_active_craft() helper. Both conclude_crafting_encounter and
auto_conclude_craft now delegate to it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: block abort during active craft in crafting encounter

abort_crafting_encounter now returns Result and rejects abort when
active_craft is Some. The player must complete or fail the craft before
aborting. Updated both call sites (action dispatch and replay) to handle
the error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: variable crafting cost distribution with RNG

Refactor crafting cost to distribute base_cost randomly across 2-4
material tokens (Ore, Plant, Lumber, Fish) using Fisher-Yates shuffle.
Constraints: max 75% of base_cost per token, each selected token gets
at least 1. Thread RNG through Library::add_card and all call sites.

BREAKING: Library::add_card now requires &mut Lcg64Xsh32 parameter.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: enemy card effects for all disciplines with validation

Add EnemyCardEffect references (effects: Vec<ConcreteEffect>) to enemy
card structs across all disciplines: OreCard (Mining), PlantCard
(Herbalism), FishCard (Fishing), EnemyCraftingCard (Crafting).

Move combat EnemyCardEffect registration from game_state.rs into
register_combat_cards(). Add new EnemyCardEffect entries for Mining (5),
Herbalism (2), Fishing (4), and Crafting (4) in their discipline files.

Extend Library::validate_card_effects() to validate enemy effects in
Mining, Herbalism, Fishing, and Crafting encounters.

BREAKING: OreCard, PlantCard, FishCard, EnemyCraftingCard structs now
include effects field.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Discipline enum and discipline_tags to LibraryCard

Add Discipline enum with Combat, Mining, Herbalism, Woodcutting,
Fishing, Rest, and Crafting variants. Add discipline_tags field to
LibraryCard and update add_card() to accept tags. Add
card_effects_for_discipline() method for filtering effects by
discipline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add vision/roadmap suggestions for Discipline tags and Insight effect

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement Research encounter system (roadmap step 10)

Add Research discipline with full encounter lifecycle:
- ResearchDef, ResearchCandidate, ResearchProject, ResearchEncounterState types
- Research variant in EncounterKind, EncounterState, Discipline, ActionPayload
- GameState.current_research field for persistent research project state
- register_research_cards: one Research encounter card in deck
- Actions: ResearchChooseProject (pay Insight, generate 3 candidates),
  ResearchSelectCandidate (pick candidate, set research cost),
  ResearchProgress (pay Insight, advance progress, create card on completion)
- Conclude/abort support with PlayerWon outcome
- Full replay_from_log support for all new action payloads
- Wired into action dispatch for pick, play, abort, conclude

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add 6 scenario tests for Research encounters and crafting fixes

Research encounter tests:
- scenario_research_encounter_full_loop: choose project, select candidate,
  progress, conclude, scout
- scenario_research_choose_and_swap_project: project persistence across encounters
- scenario_research_insufficient_insight: error on 0 Insight
- scenario_research_abort: abort yields PlayerWon, no penalty

Crafting fix tests:
- scenario_crafting_abort_blocked_during_active_craft: abort blocked during
  active craft mini-game
- scenario_crafting_card_deduplication: crafting increments existing card
  library count

Helper functions added: research_encounter_ids, win_combat_and_scout,
play_one_round_prefer_insight, deplete_encounters_until_research,
start_game_accumulate_insight_and_pick_research.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add vision/roadmap suggestions for Research and crafting tests

Documents findings about Research encounter card accessibility
(starts in deck, requires ~19 encounter depletes) and insufficient
Insight generation for completing full research projects.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update roadmap.md and vision.md for step 10 completion

Mark step 10 (Research encounters) as completed in roadmap.md with
implementation notes covering discipline tags, Insight effects, research
lifecycle, cost formulas, and deferred items. Add crafting fix annotations
to step 9.6.

Update vision.md with implementation notes for research encounters,
discipline tags, enemy card effect refactoring, and crafting fixes.

Update vision-roadmap-suggestions.md with step 10 context.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Updated issues

* chore: lower CI coverage threshold from 85% to 80%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: remove research-not-in-hand mentions from vision and roadmap

The research encounter card's starting position depends on the seed and
is not a design concern. Removed deck-vs-hand commentary from both files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* BREAKING: rename discipline_tags to valid_discipline_types

Rename the field on LibraryCard and all references across source code and
documentation. The new name better describes the field's purpose: it lists
the disciplines for which a card effect is valid.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* BREAKING: replace shared Insight token with per-discipline insight tokens

Add CombatInsight, MiningInsight, HerbalismInsight, WoodcuttingInsight,
FishingInsight, RestInsight, CraftingInsight, ResearchInsight token types.

Add TokenType::insight_for_discipline() and durability_for_discipline()
helper functions to map discipline context to the correct token pool.

Research now spends from the discipline-specific insight pool matching
the chosen research discipline. Combat grants CombatInsight, Rest grants
RestInsight.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* BREAKING: remove redundant discipline and tier_count from ResearchProject

The discipline is accessible via chosen_card.discipline. The tier_count
was only used to compute total_cost which is already stored directly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* BREAKING: change cap to limit gain amount, not total balance

Caps now limit the gain from a single card effect rather than capping
the total token balance. This applies to combat GainTokens effects,
rest encounter effects, and mining light-level gains.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update cap descriptions to reflect gain-limiting behavior

All cap mentions in vision.md and roadmap.md now consistently state that
caps limit the gain from a single card effect, not the total token balance.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add Insight card effect processing to all gathering disciplines

Mining, Herbalism, Woodcutting, Fishing, and Crafting now process
Insight effects via ConcreteEffect references on their card types.
Each grants the discipline-specific insight token (e.g., MiningInsight
for mining encounters).

Added effects: Vec<ConcreteEffect> to all gathering card effect structs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Generalize durability card effects to use TokenType::Durability

Card effect definitions now use the generic TokenType::Durability instead of
discipline-specific durability tokens (MiningDurability, HerbalismDurability,
etc.). The encounter context resolves the generic token to the appropriate
discipline pool at play time via TokenType::resolve_durability().

- Add Durability variant to TokenType enum
- Add resolve_durability() helper that maps Durability to the per-discipline
  token based on the current encounter's Discipline
- Update is_durability_cost() to match Durability (keeps split_token_amounts
  working)
- Update all card registrations in mining, herbalism, woodcutting, and fishing
  to use TokenType::Durability
- Update resolve functions in each discipline to resolve before applying
- Per-discipline durability token types are preserved for runtime balances
- Crafting repair logic unchanged (already uses specific discipline tokens)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add stamina-cost and health-cost starting cards to all 7 disciplines

Each discipline gains 2 new cards in the starting deck (library:1, deck:1):
- 1 stamina-cost card with above-average benefit
- 1 health-cost card with the highest benefit

Power scaling: no-cost < stamina-cost < health-cost

Combat: Stamina-cost attack (500-700 dmg, 20-40% Stamina) and
  health-cost attack (1000-1300 dmg, 20-40% Health)
Rest: Stamina-cost health recovery (600-800, 20-40% Stamina) and
  health-cost stamina recovery (900-1200, 15-30% Health)
Mining: MiningPower 700 (Stamina 100) and MiningPower 1200 (Health 150)
Herbalism: Or{4 traits} (Stamina 100) and Or{all 5 traits} (Health 150)
Woodcutting: 2 chops [8,5] (Stamina 100) and 3 chops [7,9,5] (Health 150)
Fishing: 3 values [150,450,700] (Stamina 100) and 5 values (Health 150)
Crafting: 2-material reduction 40ea (Stamina 75) and 4-material 40ea (Health 150)

Updated test assertions: total library cards 90→108, crafting cards 6→8.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* BREAKING: migrate mining/crafting encounter resolution to ConcreteEffect

Remove OreCard.damages and EnemyCraftingCard.increases legacy fields.
Mining and crafting encounter resolution now resolves ConcreteEffect
via library.resolve_effect() instead of hardcoded token amounts.
PlantCard.characteristics and FishCard.value kept as unique mechanics.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add GET /actions/possible endpoint for valid player actions

Returns currently valid actions based on game state and encounter phase.
Includes playable card IDs for EncounterPlayCard and EncounterPickEncounter.
OpenAPI documented via rocket_okapi. Scenario test added.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Release Optimized v0.1.3

* Add performance benchmarks for optimized hot paths in `perf_hotspots.rs`

* Optimize join operations in CRDTs to reduce cloning and improve performance

* Optimize RGAList by reusing cloned IDs and reserving capacity for batch operations

* Add caching for topological order in MemoryDAGStore to improve performance

* Implement feature X to enhance user experience and fix bug Y in module Z

* Add labeler configuration for automated file labeling in GitHub workflows

* Update .gitignore to exclude all performance log files and add benchmark results for performance analysis

* Update crates/mdcs-core/src/pncounter.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update .github/labeler.yml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Optimize RGAList delta application by reserving capacity based on new entries and update MemoryDAGStore to use RwLock for cached topological order.

* Refactor RGAList delta application benchmark to clear local pending delta and update GSet join method comment for clarity

* Add script for profiling full stress suite with cargo-flamegraph

This commit introduces a new script, flamegraph_benchmark.sh, which automates the process of building and profiling the full stress suite using cargo-flamegraph. The script ensures that the necessary environment is set up and requires the installation of the flamegraph tool via cargo.

* Bump versions for multiple packages to 0.1.2 and 0.1.3, update homepage links to documentation

* formatting and clippy errors

commit.message:
Debugger fixes (#173)

* Fix keyboard input not working until F5 in debugger

Intercept ShortcutOverride events for arrow/A keys in the application
event filter so focused widgets (QTreeView, QComboBox) cannot claim
them for their own navigation, preventing the subsequent KeyPress from
reaching our game controls.

Also give the game view StrongFocus and shift focus to it after bridge
creation so the model browser tree doesn't hold focus unnecessarily.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove 'Scenario:' label from debugger toolbar

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add GAE advantage column to debugger step history

Add an Advantage column between Action and Reward in the step history
tree. On each step, extract the model's value estimate and recompute
GAE advantages for all buffered steps using the standard formula:
  δ_t = r_t + γ·V(s_{t+1})·mask - V(s_t)
  A_t = δ_t + γ·λ·mask·A_{t+1}

Toolbar spinboxes for γ and λ default to the training hyperparameters
from ml_ppo.py (0.99, 0.95). Changing them triggers recomputation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add multi-select and Ctrl+C copy to step history

Change selection mode from SingleSelection to ExtendedSelection so
multiple rows can be selected with Shift/Ctrl+click. Ctrl+C copies
all selected rows as tab-separated text (all columns) to the clipboard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Renormalize probability column and add unmasked column

PROBABILITY column now excludes masked actions when computing
percentages, so visible values always sum to 100%. New UNMASKED
column shows raw softmax probabilities from the model.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Load State to debugger File menu

File > Load State (Ctrl+L) opens a .state file picker, resets the
environment, injects the loaded emulator state bytes, and steps a
noop to re-derive all wrapper state. The scenario remains active
for critics, end conditions, and objectives.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix multihead direction mask ignoring action constraints

flat_mask_to_multihead() was hardcoding the direction mask to all True,
meaning multihead models could sample any direction regardless of which
directions were actually valid. This affected training, evaluation, and
the debugger equally.

The direction mask is now the union of valid directions across all valid
action types. A direction is only unmasked if at least one enabled action
type allows it. This prevents e.g. MOVE N when only MOVE S is walkable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Print exit criteria metric value after each training leg

After each scenario in a training circuit, print the last observed
value of the exit criteria metric alongside the target threshold and
whether it was reached. Helps diagnose training progress without
needing to check tensorboard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add descriptive tooltips to γ and λ labels and spinboxes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix pylint warnings in frame_skip_wrapper

- Merge comparisons with 'not in' (R1714)
- Replace _value2member_map_ access with try/except (W0212)
- Suppress too-many-return-statements on _can_move_from (R0911)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Reform reward system: scaled penalties, beam incentives, urgency healing (#171)

Key changes:
- Replace remove_rewards() with scale_rewards(0.5) on damage frames
  (preserves learning signal while damage still dominates)
- Scale health penalty by damage amount (0.5→2.0 hearts maps to -0.25→-1.00)
- Add lost-beams penalty (-0.25) when health drops below full with sword
- Beam hit reward SMALL→MEDIUM (+0.50), add distance bonus (+0.05)
- Health gain scales by urgency (critical pickup worth more than trivial)
- Rupee reward SMALL→MEDIUM to compensate for removed item wavefront
- Reduce PENALTY_WRONG_LOCATION MEDIUM→SMALL (less exploration paralysis)
- Fix wallmaster early-return bug (return→continue)
- Remove 3 dead code constants

commit.message:
Movement invariant checker (#172)

* Add movement invariant checker and fix NES boundary prediction

- Add --invariants mode to diagnose.py that checks per-step movement
  invariants: zero-movement, missing wavefront, empty action masks,
  zero PBRS on tile change, and movement prediction mismatches.

- Fix _can_move_from() in frame_skip_wrapper.py to handle NES
  BoundByRoom (Z_01.asm:3505) which blocks movement at UW room
  edges independently of tile walkability.

- Fix _can_move_from() to handle doorway direction constraint:
  when doorway_dir != 0, NES only allows movement in that direction.

- Fix _can_move_from() for gridOffset edge case: when |gridOffset|>=4,
  NES uses ObjDir (not input direction), which may be blocked by
  BoundByRoom even though the input direction would be fine.

- Add 5 NES RAM variables to data.json: doorway_dir, triggered_door_cmd,
  cur_opened_doors, link_obj_timer, link_shove_dir.

Validated across 3 models, 30+ episodes, 0 movement-prediction-wrong.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cave exit movement: block north until Link steps away

The cave entrance tile (0xF3) is physically unwalkable in the NES —
Walker_CheckTileCollision blocks movement, but PlayerUnwalkable
(Z_07.asm:2952) triggers cave entry via CheckPassiveTileObjects.

Three changes:

1. Remove OW_CAVE_ENTRY_TILES override from is_tile_walkable() in room.py.
   Cave tiles are correctly treated as unwalkable for movement prediction.

2. Add is_cave_entry_direction() to Room and cave entry override in
   ZeldaGame.can_link_move() — allows agent to enter caves by detecting
   cave tiles as a valid action target (but not physically walkable).

3. Track just_exited_cave in FrameSkipWrapper. Set when cave-to-overworld
   transition is detected, cleared after any successful tile movement.
   When set, can_link_move(N) returns False, preventing cave re-entry.

Verified: at cave exit pos (64,77) only S is available. After one S step,
all valid directions unlock. 0 invariant violations across 3 episodes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
